sourceSummary.oneLine: AQP701 defines the company's product realization risk management process for medical devices throughout the product lifecycle.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used to identify, estimate, evaluate, control, document, review, and monitor medical device risks and residual risks as part of the risk management file and report.
keyPoints: The regulation applies to managing product risks associated with medical device use and risks to patients, users, other equipment, and the surrounding environment.
Risk management includes analysis, evaluation, control, and production or post-production information monitoring.
The risk management plan must define scope, responsibilities, review requirements, verification activities, post-production information activities, and risk acceptability criteria.
Risk control options are prioritized as inherent safety by design, precautions in the device or manufacturing process, and safety information.

Before commercial sale, the quality manager reviews whether the risk management plan is implemented, overall residual risk is acceptable, and production/post-production information methods are in place.

concepts.name: Risk management process
concepts.evidence: We establish and maintain a process for proactively identifying hazards related to medical devices throughout their product lifecycle, assessing, evaluating, and controlling associated risks, and monitoring the effectiveness of these controls.

concepts.name: Risk management plan
concepts.evidence: The development team leader establishes and records a risk management plan in accordance with the risk management process.

concepts.name: Risk management file
concepts.evidence: The development team leader establishes a risk management report (701-3) and manages the following traceability for each identified risk factor.

concepts.name: Risk analysis
concepts.evidence: Risk analysis is conducted on all medical devices manufactured by our company.

concepts.name: Risk evaluation
concepts.evidence: For each identified hazard, determine whether the risk is acceptable using the criteria defined in the Risk Management Plan (701-1).

concepts.name: Risk control
concepts.evidence: If risk reduction is required, perform the risk control activities described in Sections 5.8.2 through 5.8.7.

concepts.name: Risk acceptability criteria
concepts.evidence: Risks are classified into the following two areas: Widely acceptable area (GREEN AREA); Unacceptable areas (RED AREA).

concepts.name: Overall residual risk acceptability assessment
concepts.evidence: After all risk control measures have been implemented and verified, the quality manager uses the criteria defined in the risk management plan to determine whether the overall residual risk posed by the medical device is acceptable.

concepts.name: Production and post-production information
concepts.evidence: During the production and post-production stages, the quality manager establishes, documents, and maintains a system for collecting and reviewing information about the medical device or similar devices under production.

evidence.claim: The document is controlled.
evidence.evidenceText: Distinguishment ■ Controlled □ Uncontrolled

evidence.claim: The document number is AQP701.
evidence.evidenceText: Product Realization Risk Management Regulation Document No AQP701

evidence.claim: The current revision is Rev. No 6 dated 2024.04.01.
evidence.evidenceText: Revision date 2024.04.01. Rev. No 6

evidence.claim: Revision 6 changed the document name and number from Risk Management Regulation (AQP712) to Product Realization Risk Management Regulation (AQP701).
evidence.evidenceText: Change in document name and number Risk Management Regulation (AQP712) → Product Realization Risk Management Regulation (AQP701)

evidence.claim: Revision 6 states compliance with KGMP, EN ISO 13485:2016+A11:2021, FDA 21 CFR Part 820, and MDR (EU) 2017/745 Article 10 requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: Complete revision to comply with KGMP, EN ISO 13485:2016+A11:2021, FDA 21 CFR Part 820, and MDR (EU) 2017/745 Article 10 requirements

evidence.claim: The purpose is to identify, estimate, evaluate, control, and reduce risk factors throughout the product lifecycle for medical devices designed and manufactured by the company.
evidence.evidenceText: The purpose of this regulation is to identify, estimate, and evaluate risk factors throughout the entire product lifecycle to ensure that medical devices designed and manufactured by our company are safe for their intended use and meet expected performance requirements, and to control these risk factors and reduce the risk level to an acceptable level.
evidence.pageTarget: Product Realization Risk Management Regulation Purpose

evidence.claim: The procedure applies to risks associated with medical device use and risks to patients, users, other equipment, and the surrounding environment.
evidence.evidenceText: This procedure applies to the overall process of managing product risks associated with the use of medical devices and the risks to patients, users, other equipment, and the surrounding environment.
evidence.pageTarget: Product Realization Risk Management Regulation Scope

evidence.claim: Risk management monitoring includes analysis, evaluation, control, and production or post-production information.
evidence.evidenceText: The following elements are included in the monitoring process, and data on monitoring is analyzed in accordance with the Data Analysis Standard (AQP808). 1) analysis 2) evaluation 3) control 4) production or post-production information

evidence.claim: The risk management plan is part of the risk management file.
evidence.evidenceText: The risk management plan becomes part of the risk management file and includes at least the following

evidence.claim: The risk management plan must include a matrix showing acceptable and unacceptable combinations of occurrence likelihood and severity.
evidence.evidenceText: A matrix that shows which combinations of the likelihood of occurrence of a hazard and the severity of the hazard in the risk management report are acceptable and which are not

evidence.claim: Risk tolerance criteria are based on company policy, applicable national or regional laws, international standards, available state-of-the-art information, and involved parties' interests.
evidence.evidenceText: The criteria for risk tolerance are determined in accordance with our policy and are based on applicable national or regional laws and international standards, and refer to available information such as generally accepted state-of-the-art technologies and the interests of the parties involved

evidence.claim: Risk analysis documentation must include device description and identification, the person and organization performing analysis, and scope and date of analysis.
evidence.evidenceText: Documentation of the risk analysis and its results shall include at least the following. ① Description and identification of the analyzed medical device ② Identification of the person and organization that performed the risk analysis ③ Scope and date of the risk analysis

evidence.claim: Risk assessment may use sources including published standards, scientific and technical data, field data, usability engineering, clinical evidence, clinical trial results, expert opinion, and external quality assessment plan.
evidence.evidenceText: Information or data required for risk assessment can be obtained from the following sources. ① Published standards ② Scientific and technical data ③ Field data from similar medical devices already in use, including reported case reports ④ Usability engineering using typical users ⑤ Clinical evidence ⑥ Results of relevant clinical trials ⑦ Expert opinion ⑧ External quality assessment plan

evidence.claim: Risk control option analysis uses inherent safety by design, precautions in the medical device or manufacturing process, and safety information in order of priority.
evidence.evidenceText: The development team leader uses one or more of the risk control alternatives listed below in order of priority. ① Inherent safety by design ② Precautions taken during the medical device itself or manufacturing process ③ Safety information

evidence.claim: If required risk reduction is impossible during risk control option analysis, risk/benefit analysis of residual risk is performed.
evidence.evidenceText: If it is determined that the risk reduction required during the risk control alternative analysis is impossible, a ‘risk/benefit analysis’ of the residual risk is performed.

evidence.claim: Risk management classifies risks into widely acceptable area and unacceptable area.
evidence.evidenceText: Risks are classified into the following two areas ① Widely acceptable area (GREEN AREA) ... ② Unacceptable areas (RED AREA)

evidence.claim: Likelihood and severity stages should be set to at least three levels.
evidence.evidenceText: The stages of likelihood and severity should be set to at least three levels, and the risk tolerance standard should be determined based on the acceptable risk level for each product.

evidence.claim: Prior to commercial sale, the quality manager reviews the risk management process.
evidence.evidenceText: Prior to commercial sale of a medical device, the quality manager reviews the risk management process.

evidence.claim: Production and post-production review considers information from production, users, installation/use/maintenance personnel, supply chain, public sources, and state-of-the-art technology.
evidence.evidenceText: When establishing a system to collect and review information about medical devices, the quality manager shall consider the following. 1) Information generated during the production process and production process monitoring. 2) Information generated by users. 3) Information generated by those responsible for the installation, use, and maintenance of medical devices. 4) Information generated by the supply chain. 5) Information made available to the general public. 6) Information related to generally accepted state-of-the-art technology.

evidence.claim: The related forms are Risk Management Plan 701-1, Risk Management Evaluation 701-2, and Risk Management Reort 701-3, managed by the Development Team for 10 years.
evidence.evidenceText: 1 Risk Management Plan 701-1 Development Team 10 years 2 Risk Management Evaluation 701-2 Development Team 10 years 3 Risk Management Reort 701-3 Development Team 10 years
